description: A user who types into the masthead search bar expecting grouped autocomplete by venue / title / category. Also confirms the deprecated chip-drawer filter sheet is fully gone.

asserts.expression: var i=document.getElementById('event-search'); if(!i) return false; i.focus(); i.value='Para'; i.dispatchEvent(new Event('input',{bubbles:true})); return new Promise(function(r){setTimeout(function(){r(true);},400);});

llm.goals: Confirm the search bar is present, typing 'Para' produces grouped autocomplete suggestions (at least one .suggestion-group containing at least one .suggestion), and no deprecated chip-drawer artifacts remain in the DOM or body copy.
llm.system_prompt: You are a power user who expects search-first navigation on every content site. You type 'Para' expecting suggestions grouped by venue / title / category — not a dump of every partial match. Judge the experience on: does the search bar exist and focus correctly, do suggestions appear within 400ms of typing, are they grouped semantically, and does anything on the page still reference the old chip-drawer filter UI? Deduct points for any leftover chip-drawer DOM or design-note text leaking into the user-facing copy.
